channel.title: DL on Q.Docs
channel.description: Recent content in DL on Q.Docs

item.title: Concepts
item.description: Universal Approximation Theorem: A neural network with 1 hidden layer can approximate any continuous function for inputs within a specific range.

item.title: Layer
item.description: A layer is fundamentally a function that transforms input $X$ into output $Y$. This page assumes no activation. Basics Linear $$ Y=XW^T+\textbf{b} $$

item.title: Activation
item.description: An activation function adds nonlinearity to the output of a layer (linear in most cases) to enhance complexity. ReLU and Softmax are SOTA.

item.title: Toolbox
item.description: This page is a collection of tools that are widely used in model construction and experimentation, including Regularization Optimization Activation Regularization Regularization reduces overfitting by adding our prior belief onto loss minimization (i.

item.title: GNN
item.description: This page is heavily conditioned on the course “ESE 5140 Graph Neural Networks” by Prof. Alejandro Ribeiro at UPenn. Graph Def: a triplet $\mathcal{G}=(\mathcal{V},\mathcal{E},\mathcal{W})$
